verificationNote: Education cloud security requirements constructed from PDPA 2010, Education Act 1996, NACSA Act 854, and education sector cloud best practices. These are indicative -- verify against current MOE/MOHE directives and NACSA sector lead guidance.
applicableStandards: PDPA 2010 (Act 709) -- student personal data protection (especially minors)
CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 (CCM v4)
Private Higher Educational Institutions Act 1996 (Act 555) -- private university governance
MOHE IT security guidelines for public universities

studentDataProtection.description: Student data includes academic records, financial information, disciplinary records, health data, and personal identifiers. Minor student data requires enhanced protection under PDPA. Cloud hosting must address consent, purpose limitation, and data minimisation.
pdpaRequirements: Parental/guardian consent required for processing minor student personal data (below 18)
Purpose limitation -- student data may only be used for educational purposes unless separate consent obtained
Data minimisation -- only necessary student data stored in cloud services
Access rights -- students and parents have right to access personal data under PDPA s12
Retention limitation -- student records retained only for required period per Education Act requirements
cloudRequirements: Student data encrypted at rest and in transit in all cloud services
Role-based access control separating academic, administrative, and research access
Audit logging of all access to student personal data with minimum 7-year retention
Data residency in Malaysia for student records per PDPA cross-border transfer provisions
Anonymisation of student data used for institutional analytics and research

keyRequirements.name: Student data protection in cloud learning platforms
keyRequirements.description: Cloud-hosted learning management systems (LMS), student information systems (SIS), and assessment platforms must protect student personal data per PDPA requirements. Enhanced protection for minor students.
implementation: Encrypt all student data at rest and in transit
Implement RBAC separating instructor, administrator, and student access levels
Obtain parental consent for cloud processing of minor student data
Ensure LMS vendor complies with data processing agreement requirements
Regular access reviews for staff accessing student information systems

keyRequirements.name: Research data security in cloud computing
keyRequirements.description: Research data including intellectual property, grant-funded research, and sensitive datasets must be secured in cloud environments. Research computing workloads may process classified or restricted data.
implementation: Classify research data by sensitivity and apply appropriate cloud security controls
Dedicated cloud accounts or projects for research workloads separated from administrative systems
Data sharing agreements for collaborative research using cloud platforms
Encryption and access controls for research datasets in cloud storage
Compliance with grant funding body data management requirements

keyRequirements.name: Online learning platform availability and resilience
keyRequirements.description: Cloud-hosted online learning platforms must maintain high availability during examination periods and peak usage. DDoS protection and auto-scaling required.
implementation: Multi-AZ deployment for learning platforms with automatic failover
CDN distribution for learning content delivery
DDoS protection for internet-facing learning platforms
Auto-scaling configured for peak examination and enrollment periods
DR plan with tested RTO/RPO for critical academic systems

keyRequirements.name: Institutional identity and access management
keyRequirements.description: Federated identity management for students, faculty, and staff across cloud services. Integration with institutional directory services and single sign-on.
implementation: Federated identity with institutional IdP for all cloud services
MFA for administrative and faculty accounts accessing student data
Automated account provisioning and deprovisioning aligned with enrollment lifecycle
Guest and visiting researcher access management with time-limited credentials
Privileged access management for IT administrators

nacsaObligations.description: NACSA obligations for NCII-designated education infrastructure using cloud services.
nacsaObligations.applicability: Applies to education infrastructure designated as NCII under NACSA Act 854. National education information systems, major university platforms, and critical research infrastructure may be designated.
obligations: s18: Cloud-hosted education NCII must have appropriate cybersecurity measures
s21: Annual risk assessment covering cloud-hosted education systems
s23: Biennial audit including cloud security posture
s26: 6-hour notification for significant cloud security incidents affecting NCII education systems

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Ransomware targeting cloud-hosted student information systems
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Academic disruption; inability to access grades, enrollment, and financial records

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Student data breach from misconfigured cloud storage
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: PDPA breach notification; student privacy violation; institutional reputation damage

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Research data theft targeting cloud-hosted intellectual property
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Intellectual property loss; competitive research disadvantage; grant compliance violation

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: DDoS attack on online examination platform during peak period
sectorSpecificThreats.impact: Examination disruption; academic integrity concerns; student welfare impact
